Craftsmanship of
One to Many
Selling
Presentations
How to Assemble the Most Effective
Group Selling Presentations for
In-Person Delivery or Media
Module 	3: 	From the Middle to the 	Close
With 	Dan Kennedy
www.gkic.com
www.getwsodownload.com

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	4
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
One to Many 	Selling 	Presentations: From 	the Middle to the
Close
Demonstration
There are basically five kinds of demonstration that we can do.
There is the 	demonstration of 	profound importance.
For example, with Houdini’s demonstrations, a 	strait jacket escape is 	a 	strait
jacket escape is a strait jacket escape; 	it is actually easier to do it hanging from a
crane from a distance where nobody can get a good look at you than it is to do it
on stage standing in front of 	people. But think how much cooler it is to see while
you’re hanging from a crane.
So 	there 	is 	a 	way 	to 	do
demonstrations that make them much
more 	important 	that 	do 	not 	really
affect the nature of the demo.
If you’re going to show a video clip of
you getting off of an airplane, here’s
how to make that much more important 	– 	there’s a crowd of 100 people there
waiting for you to get off the plane.
That’s what they do for politicians; a politician 	is flying into a city in order to go
raise some money and they round up 100 people 	and 	take them out so the news
cameras see a big crowd waiting for the person to get off the plane.
You might have a crowd you can pull together 	or 	you can always 	rent a crowd,
but if you’re going to show that anyway, 	you might as well show it in a way
that’s important.
Demonstration Of
 	Profound Importance
 	Product Performance
 	Personal Wizardry
 	Safety in Numbers
 	“Happy” Outcomes
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	5
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
Product performance. 	Having people open envelopes with checks that have
come in is a 	way to do product performance, even when we don’t have a product
like that that actually performs.
Personal wizardry, in our world that’s often done with hot seats. Jay 	Abraham,
Garry Halbert, 	and 	me, 	we will demonstrate the wizardry by doing the hot seat
and do four, 	five people and do their businesses.
It’s 	important to pick them. You do not just let anybody stand up and come up
and do the hot seat. 	If 	you’re doing it for this purpose, it’s important to screen
them and pick them in advance.
When the faith healer smacks him on the head and they can walk and dance
again, they were pre-chosen.
It’s not anybody who wants to; 	they screen them back there and they let them
come out because they have to be 	susceptible.
In 	our case, 	if we’re going to do a hot seat, 	they have to be sane and rational and
have something that actually can be helped. 	When 	you have no control over it,
then it backfires.
I did 	a Dottie 	Walters 	seminar for speakers and 	authors. She wanted me to do
hot seats and I didn’t get a chance to screen them. So the first one is a very nice
lady who has written six books of poetry that are
now stacked up in her garage. She wants to be
the next 	“Chicken Soup For The Soul” 	with her
books of poetry that are already stacked up in
her garage, and thinks associations are going to
sponsor her and hire her.
I was much younger 	and 	I wasn’t as nice as I am now. 	So 	I did a legitimate hot
seat and she ran from the room weeping and was curled 	up outside with people
consoling her. It cast a pall 	over the rest of 	the event.
You do not just let
anybody stand up
and come up and do
the hot seat.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	6
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
My 	version of it in the Peter Lowe 	speech was of course the 	Giorgio 	letters
demonstration.
We read parts of them, we showed you how the three-step letters sequence
works. A 	lot of people haven’t seen it because I only did 	it 	when I did three-hour
long 	Magnetic 	Marketing 	presentations, 	which 	were 	mostly 	association
speaking gigs.
On stage, 	I would have a table with a couple of chairs and I would fairly early
bring a couple of 	people 	up 	out of the audience.
I would have a fill-in-the-blank template, 	right out of the 	Magnetic Marketing
system, 	of the customer reactivation letter. 	I would sit them down at the table
and have them fill in the 	blanks about their business right in front of everybody
with no preparation.
Then I would show their letters, 	thereby demonstrating that you can take this
home and you don’t have to know anything; 	all you have to do is understand
your business and you can fill in these 	blanks and 	“Bingo!” 	you 	have a letter
ready to go.
I couldn’t do it in the timeframe I had 	at Peter’s 	but I could do it if I had 300
insurance agents at 	a 	Florida State Association Insurance meeting.
There’s demonstration of 	safety 	in numbers 	– 	pictures, video, huge crowds.
There is a belief for presenters, 	speakers, 	that if they 	speak
to big audiences they are more important, smarter 	and more
valuable than if they speak to small audiences.
It’s obviously stupid but still people see video or photo of you.
You’ll notice in all of our 	GKIC 	stuff, 	we have this one wide angle shot of the
audience from the back; 	it is shot in 	an arc like the new curved 	TVs so it makes 	it
look even bigger than it really is.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	7
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
I’m 	not 	any 	smarter because I’m standing in front of that than if I 	was standing
in front of 12 people but people think 	they are.
Then there is demonstration of 	happy outcomes 	which mostly has to be done
through social proof and lifestyle visuals and so forth.
Those 	are the five types 	of demonstration. 	If you are doing 	One to 	Many
presentations that you are delivering live or by video, 	the more demonstration
you do, 	the better that you will do.
Structure of Promises
I’m 	still in the 	Middle of the presentation by the way just in case anybody got a
little lost. 	So, how do we structure promises?
Big vs Small
The first issue is how big a promise are you going to make?
I’ve two corporate clients 	who have 	both had this conversation very recently
with me of, “Can’t we scale down
the promises; 	can’t we back off the
promises?”
Yeah, 	as long as you’re willing to
sell 	a lot 	less, 	you can.
Otherwise, 	people don’t just want
to lose weight, 	they want to lose
weight 	while 	they 	sleep 	after
having eaten everything they ever
wanted to eat. 	That’s how people
want to lose weight.
So 	the 	bigger 	the 	promise, 	the
bigger the result, and there’s a huge difference.
The 	examples 	here 	are 	all actually real examples. These 	were all split tested.
BIG Promises Vs. SMALL Promises
Learn the secrets of people who never get
sick
Learn how to boost your immune system
Grow twice the garden for half 	the cost 	–
and make your neighbors Green With
Envy!
50 tips for a more bountiful garden
Foil even professional burglars and
thieves so they can never find your
valuables
The 5 best hiding places in your home for
your valuables
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	8
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
There’s an enormous difference 	– 	and 	it should be obvious 	– 	between “Learn 	the
Secrets of People Who Never Get Sick,” 	versus 	“Learn 	How to Boost Your
Immune System.” 	If 	you let the academics write 	it 	or you let the lawyers write 	it,
they will write the second one. 	If 	you have any doubt, 	in a split test, 	the first one
wins.
“Grow 	Twice The Garden for Half the Cost and Make Your Neighbors Green With
Envy,” 	or “50 Tips for a More Bountiful Garden.” 	Obviously 	we know which one
wins. 	The first one even has the prime benefit in it which is sticking it up 	the
butt of the 	neighbor.
So 	big promise versus small promise. The bigger the bolder the better.
You want to structure 	the
biggest promise that you feel
you can fulfill and won’t put
you in jail.
Direct vs Implied
Direct 	promises 	versus 	implied
promises. 	There’s 	a 	way 	to 	do
promises 	that 	are 	direct 	if 	you’re
willing to do them.
“Make 	$4000 a Day Sitting at Your
Kitchen 	Table 	in 	Your 	Underwear,”
that’s 	a 	direct 	promise 	and 	it 	has
issues.
The implied promise version of it is,
“There 	Are 	People 	Making 	Amazing
Incomes 	with 	a 	New 	Kitchen 	Table
Business.’”
DIRECT Promises Vs. IMPLIED Promises
Live At Least 15 Years Longer Than Your
‘Life 	Expectancy’ 	– 	and stay out of the
hospital, too
Forbidden Cures! 	– 	Amazing, Proven Home
Remedies That Heal Without Drugs Or
Doctors
You Can Get Very Rich, Very Fast Too 	- 	If
You’ll Join Those ‘In The Know’ About ‘The
New Frontier In America’
There Is A “New Frontier” In America 	– 	and
it is making some who are ‘in the know’
very rich, very fast
Make $4,000 A Day, Sitting At Your Kitchen
Table In Your Underwear
There Are People Making Amazing
Incomes 	With A New ‘Kitchen Table
Business’
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	9
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
Now it’s 	not as strong but it does not say, “You will” 	or 	“You can” 	or even 	“You
can potentially.”
It just states a fact and requires you to translate in your head, “If 	there are
people, 	then 	I could.”
You 	have to decide whether you want to make a direct promise or whether you
feel you need to make an implied promise.
Implied Promises as Questions
Another way to do implied promises that you will see done a lot 	is 	you will see
implied promises framed as questions.
In 1977, 	being 	a millionaire 	in 	America 	was still a big deal and so this was a
headline in 	some copy for an ad
I 	wrote 	that 	was 	extremely
effective, and the 	headline, the
question 	is, 	“YOU 	– 	A
Millionaire?”
Then there’s statements of fact
and 	then 	there’s 	questions.
Nowhere 	did I make 	a 	direct
promise.
I 	didn’t 	say 	you 	could 	be 	a
millionaire; 	would 	be 	a
millionaire; 	might 	be 	a
millionaire; 	potentially could be
a millionaire.
I 	raised 	questions 	and 	made
factual statements. 	Is 	it as strong? No.
IMPLIED Promises Framed As Questions
YOU 	– 	A Millionaire? 	There’s a New
Millionaire Explosion going on 	in America
right now. Even in this terrible economy,
more “ordinary” men and women became
New Millionaires in 2013 than any prior
year…
“Can You Write A Simple Sales Letter?
Answer yes and you could be in demand,
earning great money, writing just a few
hours a day, from anywhere in the world…
Has it occurred to you? 	– 	there must be a
better way!
Have you ever noticed there seem to be
some people who can eat whatever they
want, as much as they want 	– 	and never gain
weight?’
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	10
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
This 	is one of AWAI’s 	“Can You Write a 	Simple Sales Letter? 	Answer yes and you
could be in demand 	earning 	great money writing.”
“Have you ever noticed there seem to be some people who can eat whatever
they want, 	as much as they want, 	and never gain weight?” 	It’s 	an 	implied
promise but we didn’t say you could; 	we just said, “Have you ever noticed…?”
Primary vs Secondary
There are primary promises and secondary promises.
The primary promise is, “Sales professionals: 	you can put 	an end to cold
prospecting once and for all.”
The 	secondary promises tend to get at what does that mean to different people
who may 	be paying attention to
the primary promise.
If we were back in the 	Open, 	the
only thing you would have time to
do is the primary promise.
In the 	Middle, 	if we 	have 	properly
bought 	time 	for 	ourselves, 	we
have time to develop the idea.
So 	we can deliver the secondary
promises:
 	One 	of which has to do with
time
 	One 	of which has to do with
money
 	One 	of which has to do with
envy
PRIMARY Promises Vs. Secondary Promises
Sales Professionals: Put An End To ‘Cold
Prospecting’, Once And For All!
 	Now you can invest 100% of your time
actually selling 	– 	not hunting for
someone to sell to
 	You can double or triple your income
easily and automatically, when 	you’re
able to stop wasting time prospecting
 	You’ll be the envy of everybody else in
the office when they watch good, quality
prospects literally line up to talk to you 	–
without you ever lifting a finger, making
a cold call, suffering through another
‘mixer’ to get them
 	With the stress of never quite knowing
where your next prospect is coming
from 	– 	or when 	– 	erased, you can finally
relax, enjoy selling again, and have a
legitimate and authentic positive
attitude
 	Your spouse, kids and friends will even
notice the difference in you!
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	11
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
 	One 	of which has to do with state of mind
 	One 	of which has to 	do 	with family
So 	there’s five secondary promises for the primary promise.
I would tend usually to do three to five so here’s a couple of ways to think this
through as you go to structure 	it. 	One is primary 	promise…
 	Actual 	secondary benefits
 	“How 	I feel because of it” 	secondary benefits
 	“How 	others feel about me because of it” 	secondary benefits
This is 	a “how others feel about me” 	secondary benefit:
Your spouse, 	kids and friends will even notice the difference in you.
The people that 	sell the 	“You Can Be Home for Dinner” 	kind of thing, it’s 	“Your
dog will actually recognize you and not bite you.”
That’s how others feel about me because I have bought this, 	own 	this, 	do 	this.
So 	there’s three kinds of secondary benefits that you can build 	– 	actual 	ones,
how the buyer feels 	and how others feel about the buyer.
Another way to think about and think it through is the old wheel, this is used to
sell coaching. There’s 	primary promise and then here’s the different ways you
can do secondary promises:
 	There could be a money one
 	There 	could be a love/sex one
 	There 	could be a family one
 	There 	could be a health one
 	There 	could be a security one
 	There 	could be status one
 	There 	could 	be a lifestyle one.
You only need one of each and you’re over five.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	12
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
Stacking Promises
Another promise structure is what’s called stacking promises. 	This is important
when you’re time tight.
So, 	if 	you’re 	time 	tight 	in 	a
presentation, 	like an infomercial
or, 	for whatever reason you 	can
only do a seven and a half 	minute
video 	and 	that’s 	what 	you’ve
convinced 	yourself you can 	do,
then trying to stack two or three
promises 	into 	one 	concise
statement is what you want to do. 	Book titles often have to do this hence:
 	Napoleon 	Hill’s book title “(1) 	Grow Rich 	with 	(2) 	Peace of Mind” 	–
stacked 	title;
 	“(1) 	Win 	Friends and 	(2) 	Influence 	People,” 	stacked title.
 	Infusion’s 	“Conquer the Chaos: How to (1) Grow A Successful Small
Business (2) Without Going Crazy” 	– 	stacked title.
You do 	the same 	thing when you write headlines, 	when you write promise
statements, 	when you write claims.
You can concisely stack one, 	two or three all within one sentence.
When you’re time tight the converse truth of this is, 	if you waste a sentence
delivering only one promise, 	you probably could have delivered two promises in
the 	same sentence 	– 	if 	you sat and thought about it and worked at it.
Also 	remember that what something isn’t constitutes 	promise, 	is a structure of
promise and is often as powerful or influential 	as saying what something is.
A resource 	by the way: 	if 	you don’t own the ‘Architecture of Persuasion,’ 	by
Michael Masterson, 	you can get it at Amazon. It 	will help with promise structure.
Structure of Promises
 	BIG Promises Vs. SMALL Promises
 	DIRECT Promises Vs. IMPLIED Promises
 	IMPLIED Promises Framed As
Questions
 	PRIMARY Promises Vs. Secondary
Promises
 	STACKING Promises
 	What It ISN’T Is A Promise
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	13
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
Evergreen Pitches
Now let’s talk about 	evergreen pitches. If you’re 	going to go to the trouble to
build a pitch and you’re going to go to the trouble to build a group sales
presentation that you’re going to deliver, 	however it is you’re going to deliver it,
you might as well base it on some of the appeals that never lose their punch 	–
that 	are literally evergreen; 	they worked 	at the turn of 	the 	century, 	they’ll work
at 	the 	turn of the next century, they’ll be working long after we’re gone.
They fall into a fairly limited number of categories. 	One 	is money, 	financial
opportunity 	and the deliverable maybe changes.
You 	probably remember the little screaming guy in the infomercial with, “You
can make money with little tiny classified ads.” That same pitch today is, “You
can make money with 	Craigslist.”
There 	was 	a 	decade 	of 	very
popular, “How to make money
taking stuff to swap meets 	and
selling it.” 	That now is, “How to
make money on eBay.” 	It’s 	the
same pitch though.
So 	it’s important also when you
build your pitch today not just to
narrow your range of knowledge to what’s happened 	in the last five minutes.
Because if you will dredge historically in the category you’re in, 	you will find the
evergreen threads that have been in these pitches again and again 	and again.
As classified ads have gone away, Craigslist 	is here 	with the 	same pitch; when
Craigslist goes away, whatever the hell replaces it, it will be the same pitch.
So financial is pretty 	much evergreen, 	it never goes away; 	people 	will 	always
want more money.
Evergreen Pitches
 	Financial 	Opportunity
 	Weight 	Loss
 	Embarrassing 	Inadequacy 	Solved
 	Superiority
 	Disaster 	Prep
 	Against an Enemy
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	14
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
They’re not necessarily willing to work for more money but they always want
more money.
You will hardly ever get a hand raised if you take a survey or a poll, 	“Do you
have all the money you want and you don’t want any more?”
You may notice most of the billionaires in the country are 70 and 80 years old
and they’re working, they don’t need money but they’re still keeping score by
money.
If you put them all in a room and said, “Do you need more money?” 	most of them
would say yes; 	and the ones who didn’t say yes to 	“need,” 	if you said, “Do you
want more money?” 	they would say, “We want more money.”
Weight loss 	– 	it 	never goes away. 	People 	were fat, 	are fat 	and 	will be fat forever
more, and now it’s global because we’re exporting our food.
A 	decade ago try and find me a fat Chinese person in China, now the place is full
of them; 	they’re eating KFC, 	they’re 	eating 	Taco
Bell, 	they’re eating 	Pizza 	Hut 	– 	they’re fat. You
can see them because they all come to Disney.
It used to be you went 	to 	Disney and everybody
from 	a 	foreign 	country 	was 	skinny 	and
everybody from our country was the wide-load
people. Now everybody is a wide-load person.
You see them from the back and you think, “Well
there’s an American,” 	and you finally get past 	them and you turn around and
look at 	them, 	“Son of a gun they look like they’re from India how 	can they be
fat?”
Because we are exporting all of our shit, 	that’s why.
So 	weight loss used to be really big here, 	not very big anywhere else. 	Now 	it’s
big everywhere 	– 	and 	I do mean big everywhere.
People are 	not
necessarily willing to
work for more
money but they
always want more
money.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	15
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
Weight loss pitches haven’t changed by the way, the deliverable changes.
Embarrassing 	inadequacy 	solved 	– 	evergreen 	pitch. 	Everybody 	is 	very
insecure, 	they’re easily agitated to be more insecure 	– 	about almost anything
and everything.
Embarrassing 	inadequacy solved has been around since the beginning of time
and it will continue to be around.
The 	biography that came out last year on Dale Carnegie 	– 	“Self Help Messiah” 	–
is 	worth reading no matter what business you are in but, 	if you happen to be in
the information business, 	it’s mandatory reading.
It’s academic, 	it’s not exactly the most exciting thing you’re ever going to read.
But it is a very well-researched, 	very good book on what is the longest enduring
most successful self-help thing in America 	– 	Dale Carnegie.
The 	whole empire was all about shyness and timidity and a sense of social
inadequacy 	and, 	in 	business, 	communication
inadequacy. 	So 	it’s an inadequacy play.
Proactiv®, 	my 	client’s 	$800 	million 	a 	year
business, 	is an inadequacy play. 	It’s not as much
about what the kid sees 	or the adult sufferer 	sees
looking in the mirror for their own purposes. 	It’s
about feeling horrible about 	having acne 	because of what other people think.
Listerine’s 	ad copywriters in 1922 invented bad breath. 	There 	was no such
thing; 	it is not 	a 	medical condition. It 	was created by an advertising copywriter
trying to sell a liquid that tastes like shit. 	So they invented bad breath.
By 	the way, 	there’s a ton of those 	historical examples; 	body odor was invented
by an advertising copywriter. I don’t remember them all and I didn’t go research
them all but pretty much, 	if you name any embarrassing condition, it was
Everybody is very
insecure, they’re
easily agitated to be
more insecure.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	16
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
invented by the advertising copywriter charged with selling the solution. It had
not been previously even thought about.
Up 	until 1948 in this country, 	the average person took a shower or a bath once a
week. That was our social norm 	– 	Friday night baths. That was the social norm
in America, 	it 	started to change right around 1950.
So 	people really weren’t concerned about body odor 	until 	an advertising guy
made them concerned about body odor.
If you haven’t read the 	“Making 	Them Believe” 	book, 	everybody should; 	if you
happen to be in health care you definitely should. Brinkley really was selling the
first cure for erectile dysfunction, and that is all about sense of inadequacy so
this is an evergreen one.
Superiority 	is 	an 	evergreen 	pitch. 	Martha
Stewart exists because of the superiority appeal,
“I can be superior to my other women friends if I
have a Martha Stewart luncheon, and 	I 	make the
napkins and they’re in 	the 	shape of 	little 	owls
etc.” 	That’s 	what that is.
Cosmopolitan 	magazine 	created its cover 	and 	headline format in 1962. In 2014,
on 	the cover 	of 	the one I just saw in Barnes & Noble, one of the headlines is,
“How to 	Give Him the Best Sex he’s Ever Had.”
Men 	have 	finally bit so 	Maxim, “Mind 	Blowing Orgasms She’ll Never Forget and
No Man Will Ever Top.”
You can go back to 1962 	and 	find the same stuff on the cover of 	Cosmopolitan; 	it
hasn’t changed. For men, 	instruction 	is real; for 	women, 	pretty much all you’ve
got to do 	is 	show up naked. It’s 	not rocket science.
It’s 	about status by
purchase 	and it 	will
never go 	away.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 50 --

CRAFTSMANSHIP 	OF 	ONE TO 	MANY 	SELLING 	PRESENTATIONS 	PAGE 	17
© 2014 GKIC 	Module 3
Starbucks 	is actually 	a 	superiority play. 	People 	who are regular Starbucks
customers feel superior to schmucks who buy their coffee at Dunkin Donuts 	and
at the convenience 	store.
It’s 	a 	superiority play; 	this is about, 	“Because I buy 	this, 	I am this.” That’s what
this is about, it’s status by purchase. It 	will never go away.
Disaster prep. 	I just had lunch with 	Harry Dent and he says, “If you want
anybody to come and scare them I’m your guy. 	I can scare the shit out of them.”
So disaster prep is never going away. 	Little 	known 	Amway 	fact; 	in 1959 and
1960 	Amway 	had three products 	– 	they 	had a liquid cleaner, 	they had laundry
soap and they had backyard 	bomb shelters.
Those were the three things they were selling and they actually sold a fairly
good number of them. You 	can 	buy backyard 	bomb shelters today; 	they’re 	much
more expensive and they come and install them and they stock them with food
and air and light and water.
I’ve never understood the concept because, 	when you finally emerge, 	now what?
It’s all gone, 	you have now consumed all the water, 	all the food and you now
emerge to a crater. The only other people who are left are people who have
bomb shelters; 	they too have used everything up.
It seems to me the biggest guy 	is going to 	eat the smallest weakest guy.
Gold 	kind of goes away a little bit in cycles but it always comes out every time
there’s a bad economy.
Guns 	– 	I always say, 	if you visit somebody, 	the way to tell 	if 	they’re 	a 	Glenn Beck
listener 	is when they raise their garage door, 	half of the garage is full of gold and
half of the garage is full of guns and there’s a guy sitting in a lawn 	chair with a
gun in his lap guarding it.
If they’re not full of guns, 	it’s full of survivor food. 	So 	now you’ve 	got a Glenn
Beck 	listener. It’s a 	huge market; it 	hasn’t changed, 	it won’t go away. 	It 	ebbs and
www.getwsodownload.com
